descriptions: The Carpino killed two alleged affiliates of the Tratraculo cosca in 2006.
edges.evidence_quote: vengono uccisi i fratelli Ettore e Angelo Talarico presunti affiliati alla cosca "Tratraculo"

Kantō-kai.description: The Kantō-kai was a Japanese underworld organization formed by Yoshio Kodama in 1964, intended as a secret national police force to advance far-right views. It drew most of its membership from the Kantō region. The group disbanded in January 1965 after only fifteen months, but was a crucial step in uniting post-war gangs into the modern yakuza.

descriptions: Inagawa-kai's founder headed the Kanto-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1960s, he headed the short-lived Kanto-kai, a federation of Kantō region gangs organized by Yoshio Kodama.

descriptions: Matsuba-kai was a member group of the Kanto-kai coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization was formed of seven yakuza groups (including the Sumiyoshi-kai and Matsuba-kai)

descriptions: Sumiyoshi-kai was a member group of the Kanto-kai coalition.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization was formed of seven yakuza groups (including the Sumiyoshi-kai and Matsuba-kai)

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi refused to join the Kantō-kai, indicating a lack of cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Kodama had originally envisioned a Japan-wide gangster society, but in 1963 Kazuo Taoka and his Kansai -based Yamaguchi-gumi gang refused to join, leaving Kodama with a Kantō-heavy organization.

descriptions: Taoka refused to join the Kanto-kai, indicating non-cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: he notably refused to join the Kanto-kai, an inter-yakuza confederation in 1963.

descriptions: Frente Democrático Anticomunista y Antiterrorista continued the practices of la 42.
edges.evidence_quote: the Frente Democrático Anticomunista y Antiterrorista , also known as La Banda Colorá , continued the practices of la 42

'Ndrina Giglio.description: The Giglio are a 'ndrina (criminal clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Strongoli, Calabria, Italy. They are involved in international drug trafficking, usury, arms trafficking, money laundering, and control of the local economy. They have been allied with other 'ndrine such as Grande Aracri and Farao-Marincola. The clan was involved in a feud with the Valente family in the early 2000s and was targeted in the 2018 Operation Stige.

descriptions: The Acri clan was involved in a massacre in Strongoli in conflict with the Giglio clan.
edges.evidence_quote: È stato implicato anche nella famigerata strage di Strongoli avvenuta nel 2000 in questo paese in provincia di Crotone dove furono ammazzati a colpi di kalashnikov 5 persone in contrasto con i Giglio di Strongoli.

descriptions: The Giglio are allied with the Farao-Marincola.
edges.evidence_quote: Sarebbero alleati dei Grande Aracri e dei Mannolo di Cutro , dei Farao-Marincola di Cirò e dei Levato.

descriptions: The Giglio are allied with the Grande Aracri.
edges.evidence_quote: Sarebbero alleati dei Grande Aracri e dei Mannolo di Cutro , dei Farao-Marincola di Cirò e dei Levato.

descriptions: The Giglio are allied with the Mannolo.
edges.evidence_quote: Sarebbero alleati dei Grande Aracri e dei Mannolo di Cutro , dei Farao-Marincola di Cirò e dei Levato.

descriptions: The Giglio and Valente were formerly allies but became rivals in a feud starting in 2000.
edges.evidence_quote: Nell'anno 2000 esplode una faida a Strongoli , tra le 'ndrine dei Giglio e dei Valente prima alleate ora in contrasto per il traffico di droga, di armi e delle estorsioni .

descriptions: The Giglio are allied with the Levato.
edges.evidence_quote: Sarebbero alleati dei Grande Aracri e dei Mannolo di Cutro , dei Farao-Marincola di Cirò e dei Levato.

Hop Sing Tong.description: The Hop Sing Tong is a Chinese American tong established in 1875. It operates multiple branches across the United States, including in cities such as San Francisco, Los Angeles, and Seattle. The organization is historically associated with Chinese immigrant communities and has been involved in both legitimate and illicit activities.

descriptions: Hop Sing Tong is listed as an ally of the Black Dragons in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Wah Ching , Vietnamese Boyz Gang, Oriental Lazy Boyz , Asian Boyz , Hop Sing Tong, United Bamboo Triad, 14k Triad

descriptions: Hop Sing Tong was in conflict with Bing Kong Tong during a Tong war.
edges.evidence_quote: the Hop Sing and Suey Sing Tongs allied against the Bing Kong Tong, instigating one of the most violent Tong wars

descriptions: The infobox lists Hop Sing Tong as a rival of the Joe Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Wah Ching , Hop Sing Tong

descriptions: Infobox lists Hop Sing Tong as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K, Hop Sing Tong, Suey Sing Tong, Sun Yee On, Tiny Rascal Gang, Bloods

descriptions: The Wah Ching were absorbed into the Hop Sing Tong.
edges.evidence_quote: By approximately 1968, the Wah Ching were absorbed into the Hop Sing Tong

descriptions: La Familia has connections with the Flores Camargo cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: La Familia has certain connections with the Flores Camargo cartel

descriptions: La Nueva Administración is a splinter group of Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: La Nueva Administración en Morelos y Ciudad de México.

descriptions: Mascot Street Killas are listed as allies of Lucerne Street Doggz.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mascot Street Killas

descriptions: These three sets formed the ATF alliance together.
edges.evidence_quote: the Acacia Blocc Compton Crips, Farm Dog Compton Crips and Spook Town Compton Crips were united in an alliance called "ATF".

descriptions: These three sets formed the ATF alliance together.
edges.evidence_quote: the Acacia Blocc Compton Crips, Farm Dog Compton Crips and Spook Town Compton Crips were united in an alliance called "ATF".

descriptions: Infobox lists Acacia Blocc Compton Crips as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Acacia Blocc Compton Crips

edges.target: Brigade of the Trustees of Raqqa
descriptions: Brigade of the Trustees of Raqqa was a local affiliate of Ahrar al-Sham.
edges.evidence_quote: Ahrar al-Sham established a local affiliate known as Brigade of the Trustees of Raqqa

Swamp Angels.description: The Swamp Angels were a New York City waterfront street gang active from the 1850s to the 1890s. Based in a rookery called Gotham Court on Cherry Street in Lower Manhattan, they used the sewers to raid East River dockyards and sell stolen cargo quickly. The gang was predominantly Irish and Irish-American. They eventually merged with rival gangs to form the White Hand Gang.

descriptions: Swamp Angels merged with rival waterfront gangs into the White Hand Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: until eventually they merged with the rival waterfront gangs into the White Hand Gang at the end of the 19th century.

descriptions: Dattilo is listed as an ally of Giampà in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Dattilo, Iannazzo

descriptions: Dattilo is listed as an ally of Iannazzo.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: ... Dattilo

Cortesi brothers.description: The Cortesi brothers were four Italian-descended siblings who operated as gangsters in London in the early 20th century. They engaged in protection racketeering of gamblers and bookmakers, and were rivals of the Sabini family. The brothers were involved in a violent confrontation at the Fratellanza Social Club in 1922, leading to arrests and convictions. The group was active from the 1900s until the deaths of the brothers between 1938 and 1972.

descriptions: Initially close, then became rivals over proceeds from bookmaking and gambling clubs.
edges.evidence_quote: They were close to criminals in the English Italian Sabini family. However, the two families became rivals because the Cortesi brothers believed they were being denied a fair share of the proceeds from bookmaking and West End gambling clubs.

descriptions: Cortesi brothers challenged Sabini's power, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: Sabini managed to fend off challenges from rival gangs such as the Cortesi brothers from Saffron Hill

descriptions: Los Monguis are aligned with Los Chapitos.
edges.evidence_quote: two members of 'Los Monguis', a group aligned with Los Chapitos, were killed

descriptions: TMD and Hackney Boys were in conflict from 1997 to 2003.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1997, TMD had a conflict with another local gang known as the Hackney Boys (also known as Hackney Mandem, or HMD).

descriptions: Many Rock Machine members were formerly in the Merciless Riders.
edges.evidence_quote: The majority of the Rock Machine were former members of the Merciless Riders, the Executeurs and the SS.

Al-Qaeda.description: Al-Qaeda is a pan-Islamist militant organization founded in 1988 by Osama bin Laden and other veterans of the Soviet-Afghan War. It aims to unite the Muslim world under a caliphate and has conducted major attacks including the 1998 U.S. embassy bombings and the September 11 attacks. Designated a terrorist group by the UN and many countries, it operates globally with affiliates in various regions. Its current leader is Saif al-Adel.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda provided initial funding to Abu Sayyaf.
edges.evidence_quote: The government noted that initial funding for ASG came from al-Qaeda through the brother-in-law of Osama bin Laden, Mohammed Jamal Khalifa.

descriptions: A top al-Qaeda leader co-founded Ahrar al-Sham, indicating a structural link.
edges.evidence_quote: Abu Khalid al-Suri, a 'top al-Qaeda leader', co-founded Ahrar al-Sham

descriptions: Al-Nusra broke ties with Al-Qaeda in 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 2016, Al-Nusra announced that it was breaking ties with Al-Qaeda

descriptions: Al-Nusra was the Syrian affiliate of Al-Qaeda from 2013 to 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 2013, Al-Nusra Front was publicly confirmed as the official Syrian affiliate of Al-Qaeda

descriptions: Al-Nusra pledged allegiance to Al-Qaeda, making it the official Al-Qaeda branch in Syria.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Julani rejected the proposed merger and affirmed the group's allegiance to Al-Qaeda and its leader, Ayman Al-Zawahiri.

descriptions: Al-Nusra was an official branch of Al-Qaeda until July 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: Jabhat Al-Nusra was an official branch of Al-Qaeda.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Abu Sayyaf (pledged allegiance to ISIL in 2014)

descriptions: Al-Mourabitoun was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Mourabitoun (joined JNIM in 2017)

descriptions: Al-Nusra Front was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Nusra Front (dissolved in 2017, merged with other Islamist organizations to form Hay'at Tahrir al-Sham and split ties)

descriptions: Jabhat Al-Nusra was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Qatar financed al-Qaeda's enterprises through al-Qaeda's former affiliate in Syria, Jabhat Al-Nusra.

descriptions: Al-Nusra Front is linked to al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: al-Qaeda linked Al-Nusra Front

descriptions: Al-Qaeda launched its Iraqi regional branch, Al-Qaeda in Iraq.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2004, al-Qaeda launched its Iraqi regional branch.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda in Iraq is a franchise of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Nuaimi 'has facilitated significant financial support to al-Qaeda in Iraq'

descriptions: Al-Qaeda forces were embedded in Jama'at Al-Tawhid wal-Jihad.
edges.evidence_quote: In Iraq, al-Qaeda forces loosely associated with the leadership were embedded in the Jama'at Al-Tawhid wal-Jihad group commanded by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi.

descriptions: AQAP is a direct affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda has the following direct affiliates: - Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula (AQAP)

descriptions: AQAP is a franchise of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Humayqani exploited his role in Alkarama to fundraise on behalf of al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula (AQAP).

descriptions: AQIS is a direct affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda has the following direct affiliates: - Al-Qaeda in the Indian subcontinent (AQIS)

descriptions: AQIM is a direct affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda has the following direct affiliates: - Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb (AQIM)

descriptions: Al-Qaeda likely approved the group's name and franchised its label to AQIM.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamist rebels in the Sahara calling themselves al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb stepped up their violence in the years before 2009. French officials say the rebels have no real links to the al-Qaeda leadership, but this has been disputed. It seems likely that bin Laden approved the group's name in late 2006, and the rebels 'took on the al-Qaeda franchise label', almost a year before the violence began to escalate.

edges.target: Al-Qaeda in the Lands Beyond the Sahel
descriptions: Al-Qaeda in the Lands Beyond the Sahel was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda in the Lands Beyond the Sahel (inactive since 2015)

descriptions: Al-Shabaab harbored al-Qaeda leaders and declared allegiance, but al-Qaeda largely ignored them.
edges.evidence_quote: Somali Islamist organization Al-Shabaab occasionally harboured al-Qaeda leaders in Somalia, in exchange for their technical assistance. In September 2009, in a video entitled 'At Your Service Osama', Al-Shabaab members publicly declared allegiance to bin Laden—but this reflected an 'unrequited courtship', largely ignored by al-Qaeda.

descriptions: Al-Shabaab is a direct affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda has the following direct affiliates: - Al-Shabaab

descriptions: Ansar Al-Furqan is a non-state ally of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Ansar Al-Furqan

descriptions: Ansar al-Islam was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Ansar al-Islam (majority merged with ISIL in 2014)

descriptions: Ansar Dine was reported as an ally of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: In Mali, the Ansar Dine faction was also reported as an ally of al-Qaeda in 2013.

descriptions: Ansar Dine was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Ansar Dine (joined JNIM in 2017)

descriptions: Al-Qaeda declared war on the Badr Brigades in Iraq.
edges.evidence_quote: In Iraq it has openly declared war on the Badr Brigades

descriptions: Boko Haram was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Boko Haram (pledged allegiance to and as part of ISIL from 2014 to 2015)

descriptions: Caucasus Emirate was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Caucasus Emirate (inactive by 2015)

descriptions: Egyptian Islamic Jihad merged with al-Qaeda in the mid-1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: the merger of the overseas branch of Egypt's Al-Jihad, which was led by Ayman al-Zawahiri, with the groups bin Laden brought under his control after his return to Afghanistan in the mid-1990s.

descriptions: Fatah al-Islam is allied with al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: The following are presently believed to be allied with al-Qaeda: - Fatah al-Islam

descriptions: Haqqani network is a non-state ally of al-Qaeda.

descriptions: Harkat-ul-Jihad al-Islami is a non-state ally of al-Qaeda.

edges.evidence_quote: Ilyas Kashmiri who was the chief of Harkat-ul-Jihad Al-Islami, a Kashmiri militant group associated with al-Qaeda.

edges.evidence_quote: Harkat-ul-Mujahideen

edges.evidence_quote: By 2001, Kashmiri militant group Harkat-ul-Mujahideen had become a part of the al-Qaeda coalition.

descriptions: Hezbollah is sometimes an ally of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Hezbollah (sometimes)

descriptions: Islamic Jihad Union is allied with al-Qaeda.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda in Iraq (became the Islamic State of Iraq, which later seceded from al-Qaeda and became ISIL)

descriptions: ISIL and al-Qaeda were former allies but later became divided.
edges.evidence_quote: condemned al-Zawahiri for not making this same claim for Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi. Al-Zawahiri was encouraging factionalism and division between former allies of ISIL such as the Al-Nusra Front.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda affiliated branches joined forces with ISIS in Libya.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 2017, spokesman of the Libyan National Army (LNA) colonel Ahmed Al Masmary claimed that 'branches of the Muslim Brotherhood affiliated to al-Qaeda' had joined forces with ISIS in Libya.

descriptions: Islamic State of Iraq is linked to al-Qaeda in Iraq.
edges.evidence_quote: In November 2010, the militant group Islamic State of Iraq, which is linked to al-Qaeda in Iraq, threatened to 'exterminate all Iraqi Christians'.

descriptions: Jaish-e-Mohammed is allied with al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: The following are presently believed to be allied with al-Qaeda: - Jaish-e-Mohammed

descriptions: Jaish-e-Mohammed and al-Qaeda jointly conducted the Daniel Pearl kidnapping and murder.
edges.evidence_quote: Jaish-e-Mohammed organized the kidnapping and murder of Daniel Pearl in an operation run in conjunction with al-Qaeda and funded by bin Laden.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda has the following direct affiliates: - Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM)

edges.evidence_quote: Jemaah Islamiyah (dissolved in 2024)

edges.evidence_quote: Lashkar-e-Taiba is a member of al-Qaeda.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda was built upon the networks of Maktab Al-Khidamat.
edges.evidence_quote: Building upon the networks of Maktab Al-Khidamat, the founding members decided to create the organization to serve as a 'vanguard' for jihad.

edges.evidence_quote: Moroccan Islamic Combatant Group (dissolved)

descriptions: Rajah Sulaiman Movement was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Rajah Sulaiman Movement (leader arrested in 2024)

edges.evidence_quote: It then shifted its base to the Taliban-ruled Afghanistan

edges.evidence_quote: "Ansaru" which is loyal to al-Qaeda

descriptions: August Kreis III attempted to form an alliance between Aryan Nations and al Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2005, Kreis received media attention because he attempted to form an Aryan Nations–al Qaeda alliance.

descriptions: Boko Haram had a relationship with al-Qaeda, but al-Qaeda never officially accepted Boko Haram as an affiliate.
edges.evidence_quote: It has long been alleged that Boko Haram had a relationship with al-Qaeda.

descriptions: Camorra allegedly provided safe houses, forged documents, firearms, and explosives to Al-Qaeda in exchange for narcotics.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2005, it was alleged the Camorra created safe houses, forged documents, firearms, and explosives to Al-Qaeda in return for narcotics, which are brought into Italy via the Adriatic Sea.

descriptions: D-Company forged relationships with al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: has 'forged relationships with Islamists, including Lashkar-e-Taiba and al-Qaeda.'

descriptions: Clan members are affiliated or aligned with Al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the clan are affiliated or aligned with various Palestinian and Islamist groups, including Fatah, Hamas, Popular Resistance Committees, and Al-Qaeda.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda challenged Houthi control in Yemen.
edges.evidence_quote: While Houthi control expanded to the rest of Sanaa, as well as other towns such as Rada', this control was strongly challenged by Al-Qaeda.

descriptions: Houthis view al-Qaeda as mortal enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: the Houthis view al-Qaeda as 'Salafi jihadists' and thus 'mortal enemies'.

descriptions: Houthis accused the Saleh government of using Al-Qaeda to repress them, implying a conflict between Houthis and Al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: The Houthis in turn accused the Saleh government of being backed by Saudi Arabia and of using Al-Qaeda to repress them.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda and Islamic State entered open conflict starting February 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: 3 February 2014 : Beginning of Al-Qaeda–Islamic State conflict

descriptions: Al-Qaeda cut ties with ISIL due to conflict over al-Nusra Front.
edges.evidence_quote: In February 2014, Ayman al-Zawahiri, the leader of Al-Qaeda, announced that his group Al-Qaeda had cut ties with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant and denounced ISIL after being unable to reconcile a conflict between them and the al-Qaeda affiliate al-Nusra Front.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda disavowed relations with ISIL in February 2014, indicating a split.
edges.evidence_quote: In February 2014, after continued tensions, al-Qaeda publicly disavowed any relations with ISIL.

descriptions: Islamic State (as ISI) affiliated with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them.
edges.evidence_quote: the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency

descriptions: The Islamic State (as Islamic State of Iraq) was affiliated with al-Qaeda and fought alongside them.
edges.evidence_quote: the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda provided financial support to JTJ, the precursor to IS.
edges.evidence_quote: The financial support from Al Qaeda came after Zarqawi was reluctant to be recruited by bin Laden.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda is a non-state opponent of ISIS–K.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Non-state opponents ... al-Qaeda

descriptions: JI is alleged to have links to al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: In addition to al-Qaeda, the group is also alleged to have links to the Moro Islamic Liberation Front and Jamaah Ansharut Tauhid

descriptions: JI is allied with al-Qaeda according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Al-Qaeda, Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam (until 2009)

descriptions: The PKK lists al-Qaeda as an opponent.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... al-Qaeda

descriptions: Lashkar-e-Taiba was designated as an entity associated with al-Qaeda by the UN.
edges.evidence_quote: On 2 May 2008, it was placed on the Consolidated List established and maintained by the committee established by the United Nations Security Council Resolution 1267 as an entity associated with al-Qaeda.

descriptions: LeT cultivated links with al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: The outfit had also cultivated links with the first Taliban regime in Afghanistan and also with Osama bin Laden and his al-Qaeda network.

descriptions: LeT provides support to al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: While the primary focus for the Lashkar is the operations in Indian Kashmir, it has frequently provided support to other international terrorist groups. Primary among these is the al-Qaeda Network in Afghanistan.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda is described as an ally of the Taliban.
edges.evidence_quote: before it was overthrown by an American-led invasion after the September 11 attacks carried out by the Taliban's ally al-Qaeda

descriptions: TTP receives ideological guidance from and maintains ties with al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: The TTP receives ideological guidance from and maintains ties with al-Qaeda

descriptions: TTP has close ties to al-Qaeda, sharing resources and personnel.
edges.evidence_quote: Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan has close ties to al-Qaeda, sharing money and bomb experts and makers.

descriptions: The Musolino and Zoccali were involved in a feud that led to multiple murders.
edges.evidence_quote: La storia del " Brigante Musolino " inizia il 28 ottobre 1897 quando scoppia una rissa rusticana nell'osteria della Frasca, a Santo Stefano in Aspromonte , per una partita di nocciole: da un lato Musolino e Antonio Filastò, dall'altro i fratelli Vincenzo e Stefano Zoccali, oltre ad un loro compagno.

descriptions: Brothers for Life was closely allied with the Muslim Brotherhood Movement around 2008-2009.
edges.evidence_quote: During this time, B4L became closely allied with both MBM, and the Bandidos.

descriptions: Brothers for Life absorbed MBM, a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos had previously been aligned with Sydney's MBM gang, which had since been absorbed by B4L.

descriptions: Bakassi Freedom Fighters is the radical armed faction of BAMOSD.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha participado en acciones de secuestro y terrorismo sobre todo por el Bakassi Freedom Fighters , facción armada rádical del grupo

descriptions: Clan Fezza-De Vivo succeeded Nuova Famiglia's criminal legacy.
edges.evidence_quote: L'eredità criminale di Olivieri è stata raccolta negli anni da uomini a lui vicini, intorno ai quali si è strutturato il clan Fezza-De Vivo

'Ndrina Cicero.description: The 'Ndrina Cicero is a 'ndrina (criminal family) of the 'Ndrangheta, based in Cosenza, Calabria, Italy. It is involved in organized crime activities such as drug trafficking and extortion. Members include Domenico, Osvaldo, and Francesco Cicero. In 2011, assets worth €4 million were seized from the group.

descriptions: The 'Ndrina Cicero is a clan or 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Cicero è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese di Cosenza.

descriptions: The Cicero 'ndrina merged with the Perna and Pino-Sena.
edges.evidence_quote: A oggi i Perna dominano l'area e a queste due 'ndrine si sono uniti coloro che erano rimasti dei Perna - Cicero - Pranno e dei Pino-Sena.

descriptions: The Cicero 'ndrina merged with the Perna and Pino-Sena.
edges.evidence_quote: A oggi i Perna dominano l'area e a queste due 'ndrine si sono uniti coloro che erano rimasti dei Perna - Cicero - Pranno e dei Pino-Sena.

descriptions: The Cicero 'ndrina merged with the Perna and Pino-Sena.
edges.evidence_quote: A oggi i Perna dominano l'area e a queste due 'ndrine si sono uniti coloro che erano rimasti dei Perna - Cicero - Pranno e dei Pino-Sena.

Orekhovskaya gang.description: The Orekhovskaya gang was a powerful Russian criminal organization based in Moscow, active from the late 1980s to the early 2000s. Founded in 1988 by Sergey Timofeev, it consisted mainly of young former athletes. The gang engaged in extortion, racketeering, money laundering, and murder, and was responsible for thousands of killings. It eventually fragmented and was absorbed by the Solntsevskaya Bratva.

descriptions: The Chechen mafia came into conflict with the Orekhovskaya gang.
edges.evidence_quote: This brought them into conflict with the Slavic gangs, including the Solntsevskaya bratva and the Orekhovskaya gang

descriptions: Orekhovskaya and Solntsevskaya allied against Chechen mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 the gang merged with the Solntsevskaya bratva to combat the growing threat of Chechen mafia gangs.

descriptions: Izmailovskaya gang is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kuntsevskaya gang, Caucasian gangs, Izmailovskaya gang

descriptions: Kuntsevskaya gang is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kuntsevskaya gang, Caucasian gangs, Izmailovskaya gang

descriptions: The alliance between Orekhovskaya and Solntsevskaya ended by 1997.
edges.evidence_quote: However, by 1997 the alliance had split and Todorov was again running an independent gang.

descriptions: Orekhovskaya merged with Solntsevskaya bratva in 1989 to fight Chechen mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 the gang merged with the Solntsevskaya bratva to combat the growing threat of Chechen mafia gangs.

descriptions: Remnants of Orekhovskaya were absorbed by Solntsevskaya Bratva after the split.
edges.evidence_quote: what was left of the gang was swallowed up by the Solntsevskaya Bratva

descriptions: Solntsevskaya allied with Orekhovskaya gang against the Chechen mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Together with the Orekhovskaya gang and other Slavic mobs, the Solntsevo made an alliance to drive the Chechens out.

descriptions: Ghazi Brigade worked closely with TTP in 2009, jointly planning attacks.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2009 the Ghazi Brigade worked closely with the TTP during military operations in the Swat Valley, and the two groups jointly planned attacks on western targets in Islamabad.

descriptions: Popular Resistance of Sudan is listed as an opponent of RSF.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Popular Resistance of Sudan

descriptions: YJA-STAR is the women's armed wing of the PKK.
edges.evidence_quote: The Free Women's Units of Star (YJA-STAR) was established in 2004 as the women's armed wing of the PKK.

descriptions: Chotta Rajan was a former key aide and lieutenant of Dawood Ibrahim, leader of D-Company.
edges.evidence_quote: He was a former key aide and lieutenant of Dawood Ibrahim.
